source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Minimize cluster access to read-only for ACR

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Minimize cluster access to read-only for ACR
input_row.description: Configure	the	Cluster	Service	Account	with	Storage	Object	Viewer	Role	to	only	allow	read- only	access	to	ACR.
input_row.rationale: The	Cluster	Service	Account	does	not	require	administrative	access	to	ACR,	only	requiring pull	access	to	containers	to	deploy	onto	ACK.	Restricting	permissions	follows	the	principles of	least	privilege	and	prevents	credentials	from	being	abused	beyond	the	required	role. Impact: A	separate	dedicated	service	account	may	be	required	for	use	by	build	servers	and	other robot	users	pushing	or	managing	container	images
input_row.audit: Review	Alibaba	Cloud	ECS	master	node	and	worker	node	RAM	roles'	RAM	Policy Permissions	to	verify	that	they	are	set	and	the	minimum	required	level. Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 2. In	the	left-side	navigation	pane,	click Clusters . 3. On	the Clusters page,	click	the	name	of	a	cluster	or	click Details in	the	Actions column.	The	details	page	of	the	cluster	appears. 4. In	the	left-side	navigation	pane	of	the details page,	click Cluster	Information . 5. On	the Cluster	Information page,	click	the Cluster	Resources tab	and	click	the name	of	the**	worker	RAM	role**	or master	RAM	role on	the	tab. 6. You	are	then	redirected	to	the	RAM	console.	On	the Permissions tab,	click	the	name in	the Policy column. 7. On	the details page	of	the	policy,	click Policy	Document table	to	review	the	RAM policy	permissions. If	utilizing	a	3rd	party	tool	to	scan	images	utilize	the	minimum	required	permission	level required	to	interact	with	the	cluster	-	generally	this	should	be	read-only.
input_row.remediation: 253	|	P a g e You	can	use	your	ACR	images	with	ACK,	but	you	need	to	satisfy	the	following	prerequisites. The	ACK	master	and	worker	node	RAM	roles	that	you	use	with	your	master/worker	nodes must	possess	the	AliyunContainerRegistryReadOnlyAccess	RAM	policy	permissions	for ACR. Default	Value: These	RAM	permissions	are	applied	to	your	ACK	master	and	worker	node	RAM	roles	by default. References: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/128468.htm 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/67992.htm CIS	Controls: Version	7 3.2	Perform	Authenticated	Vulnerability	Scanning Perform	authenticated	vulnerability	scanning	with	agents	running	locally	on	each	system or	with	remote	scanners	that	are	configured	with	elevated	rights	on	the	system	being tested. 254	|	P a g e
input_row.impact: A	separate	dedicated	service	account	may	be	required	for	use	by	build	servers	and	other robot	users	pushing	or	managing	container	images
input_row.references: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/128468.htm 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/67992.htm CIS	Controls: Version	7 3.2	Perform	Authenticated	Vulnerability	Scanning Perform	authenticated	vulnerability	scanning	with	agents	running	locally	on	each	system or	with	remote	scanners	that	are	configured	with	elevated	rights	on	the	system	being tested. 254	|	P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

The control requires checking the permissions of RAM roles associated with ACK clusters to ensure they have read-only access to Alibaba Cloud Container Registry (ACR). This type of verification can be efficiently performed using Alibaba Cloud APIs or CLI tools as it involves querying and validating IAM (RAM) policy configurations, which are well-documented and supported by Alibaba Cloud's management interfaces.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ali_ack_ram_role_read_only_acr_access

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

1. **Alibaba Cloud API Calls:**
   - Use the `ram:ListPoliciesForRole` API to list the policies attached to the master and worker node RAM roles of an ACK cluster.
   - Use the `ram:GetPolicy` API to retrieve the policy document for each policy attached to these roles.

2. **Aliyun CLI Commands:**
   - Use `aliyun ram ListPoliciesForRole --RoleName <RoleName>` to list policies attached to a specific RAM role.
   - Use `aliyun ram GetPolicy --PolicyName <PolicyName> --PolicyType System` to get the details of a policy.

3. **Specific Resource Enumeration:**
   - Identify and list RAM roles associated with the ACK cluster nodes (both master and worker nodes).
   - Iterate through the RAM roles and list the policies using the APIs/CLI methods mentioned above.

4. **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Check if the `AliyunContainerRegistryReadOnlyAccess` policy is attached to the RAM roles.
   - Ensure that no policies with broader access than read-only permissions towards ACR are attached to these RAM roles.

5. **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
   - **Pass:** The RAM roles have the `AliyunContainerRegistryReadOnlyAccess` policy attached and no higher-privilege policies are present.
   - **Fail:** One or more RAM roles do not have the `AliyunContainerRegistryReadOnlyAccess` policy, or they have additional policies granting more than read-only access to ACR. 

By using the labeled commands and APIs, an automated script will effectively query the necessary policies and validate the permissions against the expected criteria. This ensures the control's compliance status can be checked programmatically without manual intervention.
